what: Replace a16wfp4_preshuffle (inline PREQUANT) with separate quant + afp4wfp4_preshuffle kernel for split-K shape (16x2112x7168). afp4wfp4_preshuffle has built-in XCD remap (remap_xcd) for 8 XCD CDNA4 locality, never tried for this shape. Quant overhead trivial (M=16, 57KB FP4). Use tuned configs from AOT: BSM=8 BSN=128 BSK=1024 NW=4 NS=2 waves=1 .cg split-K=14.
why: afp4wfp4_preshuffle_kernel only tried once (v3, early dtype error). It has XCD remap built into the kernel itself (not added externally like v114/v192 which hurt small grids). BSK=1024 halves K-loop iterations vs BSK=256. The separate quant for M=16 is <1us. 16x2112x7168 at ~11us is 1/6 of geomean.

what_failed: Session 15: 1 approach tested after deep research (227+ prior attempts reviewed). (1) Separate quant + afp4wfp4_preshuffle kernel for split-K (16x2112x7168) — XCD remap built into kernel — massive regression +6.8% (15.9us vs 11us) because extra quant kernel launch + memory traffic for A_fp4/A_scale costs ~5us which dominates. All viable Triton/ASM kernel combinations, tile configs, cache modifiers, env vars, dispatch overhead reductions, and library kernel paths exhausted across 228 attempts. The 9.5% gap to #1 (8.23us) likely requires automated hyperparameter search or fundamentally different kernel architecture.
